tasks.title: Dead code cleanup: onCommand stub and legacy schema models
tasks.description: Two cleanup items. (1) Add deprecation comments to parse-commands.ts and Steps 6-7 of handleMessage in orchestrator/index.ts documenting that onCommand is intentionally unused — all commands now use PluginTool via tool-server. Do not delete yet. (2) Delete User and Post models from packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma — Prisma template artifacts with zero application references.

subtasks.title: Mark onCommand pipeline steps as deprecated
subtasks.description: Add JSDoc/inline comments to parse-commands.ts and Steps 6-7 of handleMessage explaining this code path is unused and intentionally preserved as a stub. All commands route through PluginTool/tool-server instead.

subtasks.title: Remove User and Post models from schema
subtasks.description: Delete User and Post model definitions from packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma. Run pnpm db:push or create migration. Verify zero references to User or Post in application code before deleting.

tasks.details: Verified: onCommand (Steps 6-7 in orchestrator/index.ts) and parse-commands.ts are dead stubs — zero plugin implementations. User and Post confirmed as Prisma template artifacts with zero application references.
tasks.testStrategy: Grep for User and Post imports after deletion — confirm zero. Confirm no TypeScript errors. Confirm DB migration completes cleanly.

tasks.title: Small consistency fixes (MODEL_OPTIONS, audit URL, metrics warning)
tasks.description: Three small consistency fixes in one PR. (1) Extract MODEL_OPTIONS from manage-thread-modal.tsx and model-selector.tsx into a shared _helpers/model-options.ts. (2) Fix request-audit-delete.ts to use getOrchestratorUrl() helper instead of inline URL construction. (3) Add ctx.logger.warn in metrics plugin when inputTokens/outputTokens/model are missing from InvokeResult.

subtasks.title: Extract MODEL_OPTIONS to shared helper
subtasks.description: Create apps/web/src/app/(chat)/chat/_helpers/model-options.ts exporting MODEL_OPTIONS. Update manage-thread-modal.tsx and model-selector.tsx to import from it.

subtasks.title: Fix requestAuditDelete URL pattern
subtasks.description: In apps/web/src/app/(chat)/chat/_actions/request-audit-delete.ts, replace inline orchestrator URL construction with getOrchestratorUrl() helper.

subtasks.title: Add warning log for missing metrics fields
subtasks.description: In packages/plugins/metrics/src/index.ts, add ctx.logger.warn when inputTokens, outputTokens, or model are absent from InvokeResult and the metrics write is being skipped. Currently silent — cost cap enforcement silently fails when metrics don't write.

tasks.details: Circuit breaker verified as fully implemented (commit dfb7f55, March 1) — removed from this task. These three remain unaddressed.
tasks.testStrategy: MODEL_OPTIONS: both modal and selector show same options from one source. Audit URL: confirm fetch target uses helper. Metrics: mock a missing-field InvokeResult and verify warn log appears.

tasks.title: Add AgentConfig database model
tasks.description: Add AgentConfig model to packages/database/prisma/schema.prisma. This table stores per-agent feature flags and will hold the heartbeatCron expression when Agent Identity Phase 5 is implemented. Creating it now prevents a mid-feature schema migration when heartbeat arrives.
tasks.details: Schema: { id, agentId (unique FK to Agent), memoryEnabled (default true), reflectionEnabled (default false — Phase 4 not yet implemented), heartbeatEnabled (default false — Phase 5 blocked on cron), heartbeatCron (String? — cron expression when heartbeat is wired), createdAt, updatedAt }. Also add reverse relation on Agent model. Run db:generate after schema change.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify AgentConfig can be created/read/updated via Prisma client. Verify FK constraint with Agent enforces referential integrity.

tasks.title: Complete custom thread instructions feature
tasks.description: Thread.customInstructions field already exists in Prisma schema. UI textarea already exists in manage-thread-modal.tsx but is unwired — stale comment says 'once DB field exists' but the field DOES exist. Three changes needed: server action, UI wiring, orchestrator injection.

subtasks.title: Create update-thread-instructions server action
subtasks.description: Create apps/web/src/app/(chat)/chat/_actions/update-thread-instructions.ts. Takes threadId and customInstructions string. Calls prisma.thread.update. Same pattern as rename-thread.ts.

subtasks.title: Wire manage-thread-modal textarea
subtasks.description: In manage-thread-modal.tsx, add controlled state for the instructions field, load initial value from thread prop, call update-thread-instructions on save. Remove the stale TODO comment.

subtasks.title: Inject customInstructions in prompt assembler
subtasks.description: In apps/orchestrator/src/orchestrator/_helpers/prompt-assembler.ts, when thread.customInstructions is non-null/non-empty, append it to the assembled prompt. Position: after the kind instruction, before the base content. Format as a clearly labeled section.

tasks.details: Verified: Thread.customInstructions column exists in schema. UI field exists in manage-thread-modal.tsx with placeholder text and stale 'DB field does not exist' comment. Neither the action nor the orchestrator injection exists.
tasks.testStrategy: Set custom instructions on a thread, send a message, verify instructions appear in the assembled prompt (visible in pipeline_step activity records or via logger).

tasks.title: Implement cron scheduler as @harness/plugin-cron
tasks.description: Implement the missing executor for the cron system. Schema (CronJob), seed data (4 jobs), admin UI (/admin/cron-jobs), thread kind='cron' support, and prompt templates all exist. Only the scheduler is missing. Must be implemented as a plugin (not a top-level module) so it can be disabled via PluginConfig at runtime. ctx.sendToThread is safe to call inside plugin start() hooks.

subtasks.title: Create @harness/plugin-cron package
subtasks.description: Create packages/plugins/cron/ with package.json, tsconfig.json, and src/index.ts following the pattern of other plugins. Add to pnpm workspace.

subtasks.title: Add cron library dependency
subtasks.description: Add croner (preferred over node-cron for timezone/UTC offset support) to packages/plugins/cron/package.json. Verify compatibility with Node >= 22.

subtasks.title: Implement cron-server helper
subtasks.description: Create packages/plugins/cron/src/_helpers/cron-server.ts. On start: query CronJob where enabled=true, schedule each using its cron expression (UTC). On trigger: validate threadId is non-null, call ctx.sendToThread(cronJob.threadId, cronJob.prompt), then atomically update cronJob.lastRunAt and compute nextRunAt. Wrap execution in try/catch — update lastRunAt even on failure. On stop: clear all scheduled jobs.

subtasks.title: Implement plugin start/stop lifecycle
subtasks.description: In packages/plugins/cron/src/index.ts, implement start() hook that initializes the cron-server, and stop() hook that tears it down. No onBeforeInvoke/onAfterInvoke needed — this plugin is lifecycle-only. Use ctx.sendToThread (safe in start()) and ctx.db to query CronJob records.

subtasks.title: Register cronPlugin in plugin-registry
subtasks.description: Add cronPlugin to ALL_PLUGINS array in apps/orchestrator/src/plugin-registry/index.ts. Position: after webPlugin (web must start first so HTTP is up before any cron jobs could theoretically fire via HTTP path, though sendToThread is in-memory and safe regardless).

tasks.details: VERIFIED BOOT SEQUENCE: ctx.sendToThread is safe in start() hooks (pipeline.handleMessage is assigned before start() fires). Seeded CronJobs already have valid threadIds pointing to the primary thread. Use atomic prisma.cronJob.update() for lastRunAt/nextRunAt — no read-modify-write. Plugin pattern preferred over top-level module per architectural invariants (enables runtime disable via PluginConfig). All 4 seeded jobs use UTC schedule expressions (MST = UTC-7).
tasks.testStrategy: Create a test CronJob with a near-future schedule. Verify it fires, sendToThread is called with correct prompt, lastRunAt is updated in DB. Verify disabled jobs do not fire. Verify stop() clears all scheduled jobs cleanly.

tasks.title: Documentation: CLAUDE.md system inventory and planned-incomplete sections
tasks.description: Add two new sections to CLAUDE.md to prevent future agents from rebuilding existing systems or missing the state of planned systems.

subtasks.title: Add 'What Already Exists' section to CLAUDE.md
subtasks.description: Document every fully-implemented subsystem: admin section (/admin with cron-jobs, plugins, threads, agent-runs, tasks), agent management (/agents CRUD + memory browser), usage dashboard (/usage), agent identity system (Phases 1-2 complete: soul injection + episodic memory scoring), summarization plugin (at 50-message threshold), audit-delete flow, delegation with validator and circuit breaker, cron scheduler (after Task 5).

subtasks.title: Add 'Planned But Incomplete' section to CLAUDE.md
subtasks.description: Document: Agent Identity Phase 3 (vector search — Qdrant is the candidate, NOT pgvector, decision pending), Agent Identity Phase 4 (reflection cycle — schema REFLECTION type exists, trigger logic not implemented), Agent Identity Phase 5 (heartbeat — blocked on AgentConfig table + cron scheduler, both now exist after Tasks 3 and 5). Custom thread instructions (done in Task 4).

tasks.details: Do this LAST so docs reflect actual final state of all other tasks. The admin section and /agents route are fully implemented but mentioned nowhere in CLAUDE.md — biggest source of agent re-implementation.
tasks.testStrategy: Read CLAUDE.md after changes. Verify a new agent would know: admin exists, /agents exists, heartbeat needs cron, vector search uses Qdrant not pgvector, which identity phases are complete vs. paused.

tasks.title: Documentation: architectural rules for cron and agent identity state
tasks.description: Add rule files documenting the cron integration pattern and agent identity system state. Prevents future agents from designing these systems from scratch.

subtasks.title: Create .claude/rules/cron-scheduler.md
subtasks.description: Document: plugin pattern (not top-level module), how cron-server queries CronJob records, what nextRunAt means, how a trigger becomes a sendToThread call, thread kind='cron' behavior, admin UI at /admin/cron-jobs, the dependency chain for per-agent heartbeat (CronJob system → AgentConfig.heartbeatCron → identity plugin Phase 5). Include boot sequence note: ctx.sendToThread safe only in start() hooks.

subtasks.title: Create .claude/rules/agent-identity-state.md
subtasks.description: Document: Phase 1-2 complete (soul injection, episodic memory, importance scoring, dual injection, memory retrieval). Phase 3 paused — vector search via Qdrant (NOT pgvector) when decided. Phase 4 paused — reflection cycle (REFLECTION MemoryType exists in schema). Phase 5 paused — heartbeat blocked on AgentConfig + cron. Plugin ordering: identity runs BEFORE context in onBeforeInvoke chain (registered first in ALL_PLUGINS). AgentConfig table exists for per-agent config.

tasks.details: Do this LAST so rules reflect actual final state. These files are loaded into agent context via .claude/rules/ and directly prevent architectural drift.
tasks.testStrategy: Rules files exist, are accurate, and reference real file paths. An agent reading them should understand the cron wiring and identity phase state without reading source code.

metadata.description: Surgical fixes for codebase drift. PRD: .taskmaster/docs/prd-surgical-fixes.md. Verified by 4 deep-trace agents on 2026-03-02.

verified.activity_persistence_duplication: CLOSED — refactor complete as of sprint 4. Orchestrator writes only kind='text'. Activity plugin owns all other kinds. Zero overlap confirmed.
verified.sessionid_bug: CLOSED — fixed 2026-03-01 via commit 2251316 (feat(sprint4a): sync Thread.sessionId on session loss and rotation). Covered by 3 test cases.
verified.circuit_breaker: CLOSED — implemented 2026-03-01 via commit dfb7f55 (feat(safety): add orphaned-task recovery, cost cap, and circuit breaker). Full failure categorization, fast-fail on logic errors, quadratic backoff with jitter.
verified.cron_boot_sequence: CONFIRMED — implement as plugin (not top-level module). ctx.sendToThread safe in start() hooks. Seeded CronJobs have valid threadIds. Use croner library for timezone support.
